api/client/python/openmeter/_generated.purpose: Root of the auto-generated Python SDK for OpenMeter. Exposes OpenMeterClient (sync) as the sole public entry point; all resource groups are attributes instantiated in __init__ with a shared PipelineClient, Serializer, and Deserializer. Never edit directly — regenerated by `make gen-api`.

patterns.name: Single shared PipelineClient with fixed policy chain
patterns.description: OpenMeterClient constructs one PipelineClient with a fixed policy list (headers, user-agent, proxy, ContentDecode, retry, auth, logging) and passes it to every Operations constructor. Never create a second PipelineClient inside an Operations class.

patterns.name: Operations-as-attributes pattern
patterns.description: Every resource group is instantiated as a named attribute in OpenMeterClient.__init__ receiving (self._client, self._config, self._serialize, self._deserialize). Access via client.customers.list_customers(), not by instantiating Operations directly.

patterns.name: send_request URL formatting
patterns.description: When calling client.send_request with a raw HttpRequest, the {endpoint} placeholder in the URL must be resolved via format_url before dispatch. The method deepcopies the request and calls self._client.format_url with the serialized endpoint.

patterns.name: _patch.py customization escape hatch
patterns.description: _patch.py is the ONLY file preserved across regenerations. Add public symbols to __all__ there and implement patch_sdk() for post-generation monkey-patching. __init__.py extends __all__ from _patch_all automatically.
patterns.example: from ._patch import __all__ as _patch_all; __all__.extend([p for p in _patch_all if p not in __all__])

patterns.name: Context manager protocol on client
patterns.description: OpenMeterClient implements __enter__/__exit__ delegating to self._client. Always use with-statement or call close() explicitly to release the underlying HTTP connection pool.

patterns.name: Union type aliases in _types.py
patterns.description: _types.py defines tagged-union type aliases (e.g. App, Entitlement, RateCard) using TYPE_CHECKING guards to avoid circular imports. These are the canonical discriminated-union types used as parameter and return types across Operations methods.

key_file_guides.role: Sync entry point — constructs PipelineClient, policy chain, Serializer/Deserializer, and all Operations instances as attributes.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Generated file — any hand-edit is overwritten by make gen-api. Adding a new resource group requires regenerating, not editing here.

key_file_guides.role: Holds OpenMeterClientConfiguration: stores endpoint, polling_interval, and all corehttp policy instances. Injected into every Operations constructor.
key_file_guides.watch_for: authentication_policy defaults to None — callers must pass one via kwargs or requests will be unauthenticated.

key_file_guides.role: Only file safe to edit; survives regeneration. patch_sdk() runs after __init__.py imports complete.
key_file_guides.watch_for: patch_sdk() must remain present and callable — removing it breaks __init__.py import.

key_file_guides.role: Defines tagged-union Python type aliases for discriminated types (App, Entitlement, RateCard, NotificationRule, etc.) used as parameter/return type annotations across Operations.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Generated file. Uses TYPE_CHECKING guard to avoid circular imports — do not import these at runtime outside of type annotations.

key_file_guides.role: Pins SDK version string used by sdk_moniker in OpenMeterClientConfiguration user-agent.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Value is 0.0.0 in source; real version is injected at release build time. Do not hardcode a real version here.

key_file_guides.role: Public surface: re-exports OpenMeterClient and VERSION; merges _patch_all into __all__ and calls patch_sdk().
key_file_guides.watch_for: Generated — do not add imports here. Use _patch.py for any custom exports.

anti_patterns: Hand-editing _client.py, __init__.py, or _version.py — all are overwritten by make gen-api
Instantiating Operations classes directly instead of accessing them through the client attributes (bypasses shared serializer/deserializer and config)
Passing a raw URL string to send_request without the {endpoint} placeholder resolved via format_url — causes malformed URLs against the target endpoint
Adding retry or authentication logic inside _patch.py instead of injecting a custom policy via the policies kwarg to OpenMeterClient
Calling client.close() without first draining streaming responses — leaks HTTP connections in the underlying PipelineClient pool

decisions.decision: All request-building logic lives in child operations/_operations.py build_*_request helpers; _client.py only wires the pipeline.
decisions.rationale: Keeps the client thin and regenerable; operations contain all schema-specific serialization so regeneration scope is isolated to child packages.

decisions.decision: OpenMeterClientConfiguration is a separate injectable class rather than raw kwargs.
decisions.rationale: Allows callers to share or replace a config object (e.g. swap authentication_policy) without reconstructing the client.

decisions.decision: _patch.py is the sole file preserved across regenerations as a customization boundary.
decisions.rationale: Prevents hand-edits from being silently lost on the next gen-api run while still allowing SDK-level monkey-patching via patch_sdk().

code_examples.scenario: Instantiate the sync client with a bearer-token auth policy and call a resource group method
code_examples.code:
from corehttp.runtime import policies
from openmeter._generated import OpenMeterClient

auth = policies.BearerTokenCredentialPolicy(credential, "scope")
client = OpenMeterClient(
    endpoint="https://api.openmeter.cloud",
    authentication_policy=auth,
)
with client:
    customers = client.customers.list_customers()
